tests.name: tessellateBezier produces correct vertex count for LineSegments format (segments * 2 per pair)
tests.description: Given two 3D endpoints and a segment count of 5, the tessellation should produce 10 vertices (5 segments × 2 vertices each) in LineSegments format.
assertions: Output positions array length equals segments * 2 * 3 (vec3 per vertex)
Output aEdgeParam array length equals segments * 2
Output aArcOffset array length equals segments * 2 * 3

tests.name: tessellated curve endpoints match the original input points
tests.description: The first vertex of the first segment should match point A, and the last vertex of the last segment should match point B.
assertions: First vertex position equals point A (within floating-point tolerance)
Last vertex position equals point B (within floating-point tolerance)

tests.name: control point offset is perpendicular to the pair axis
tests.description: The arc offset vectors at the midpoint should be perpendicular to the line between A and B (dot product ≈ 0).
assertions: Dot product of aArcOffset at midpoint vertex with (B - A) direction is approximately zero
aArcOffset magnitude at midpoint is non-zero (curve actually arcs outward)

tests.name: aEdgeParam values range from 0 to 1 along the curve
tests.description: The parametric t values stored in aEdgeParam should start near 0 at the first endpoint and end near 1 at the last endpoint.
assertions: All aEdgeParam values are between 0 and 1 inclusive

tests.name: aArcOffset has bell-curve weighting (zero at endpoints, max at midpoint)
tests.description: Arc offset magnitude should be zero at endpoints and largest near the curve midpoint, following sin(t * PI) weighting.
assertions: aArcOffset magnitude at first vertex (t=0) is zero or near-zero
aArcOffset magnitude at last vertex (t=1) is zero or near-zero
aArcOffset magnitude at midpoint vertex is greater than at endpoint vertices

tests.name: tessellation with segments=4 and segments=6 both produce valid output
tests.description: The configurable segment count (4–6 per acceptance criteria) should produce correct vertex counts for both bounds.
assertions: All output arrays have consistent lengths

tests.name: degenerate input: identical points A and B produce valid output without NaN
tests.description: When both endpoints are the same, the perpendicular vector computation should not produce NaN or Infinity.
assertions: No NaN values in output positions

tests.name: perpendicular fallback works when pair axis is aligned with reference vector
tests.description: If A-B axis is parallel to the default cross-product reference vector (e.g., Y-axis), a fallback reference must be used to avoid degenerate perpendicular.
assertions: aArcOffset at midpoint has non-zero magnitude even when A-B is axis-aligned

tests.name: arcHeight parameter scales the offset magnitude
tests.description: Providing a larger arcHeight should result in proportionally larger aArcOffset magnitudes at the midpoint.
assertions: Midpoint aArcOffset magnitude with arcHeight=2 is approximately 2× the magnitude with arcHeight=1

tests.name: output is deterministic for the same input
tests.description: Calling tessellateBezier twice with the same points and parameters produces identical output arrays.

tests.name: init() adds a THREE.LineSegments mesh to the scene
tests.description: After calling init with valid positions, the scene should contain exactly one LineSegments child.

tests.name: LineSegments mesh has BufferGeometry with position, aArcOffset, aEdgeParam, and aDistance attributes
tests.description: The geometry must have all required attributes for the Bezier vertex shader.

tests.name: curves are tessellated into configurable segments (4-6) per connection
tests.description: With segments=5 and N connections, activeVertexCount should be N × 5 × 2.
assertions: activeVertexCount > 0 for inputs with nearby points

tests.name: connection pairs are selected from a subset (not all-to-all) to control density
tests.description: With many dense points, connections should be bounded by maxConnections config, not O(n²).
assertions: activeVertexCount / (segments * 2) is less than or equal to maxConnections
Connection count is significantly less than n*(n-1)/2 for large n

tests.name: material uses ShaderMaterial with transparent, additive blending, depthWrite false
tests.description: The line material must be configured for compositing as a glow layer over the point cloud.

tests.name: material has uOpacity uniform with low initial value (0.05–0.15 range)
tests.description: Per acceptance criteria, individual curve opacity must be low so overlapping density creates the web effect.
assertions: Initial uOpacity value is between 0.05 and 0.15 inclusive

tests.name: bass energy modulates arc curvature via uBassArcScale uniform
tests.description: The draw() method must update a uBassArcScale uniform based on bass energy, causing arcs to become more pronounced with stronger bass.
assertions: uBassArcScale with bassEnergy=0 is less than uBassArcScale with bassEnergy=1.0

tests.name: draw() updates standard uniforms (uTime, uBassEnergy, uTrebleEnergy, uPaletteHue)
tests.description: Frame state parameters should propagate to shader uniforms each frame.

tests.name: setOpacity updates uOpacity uniform
tests.description: The overlay's setOpacity method must update the shader uniform for mode transitions.

tests.name: cleanup() removes mesh from scene and disposes geometry and material
tests.description: Cleanup must fully release GPU resources and remove the mesh from the scene graph.
assertions: scene.children has no LineSegments after cleanup

tests.name: init with null positions does not throw (graceful skip)
tests.description: Incompatible geometry systems may pass null; the overlay must handle this gracefully.

tests.name: init with fewer than 2 points produces no connections
tests.description: A single point cannot form a pair, so no connections or mesh should be created.

tests.name: vertex shader source includes aArcOffset attribute declaration
tests.description: The custom vertex shader must declare the arc offset attribute for Bezier displacement.

tests.name: connection count adapts to maxConnections config (quality tier adaptation)
tests.description: Lower maxConnections config should produce fewer connections than higher config with the same input.
assertions: activeVertexCount with maxConnections=50 <= activeVertexCount with maxConnections=500
Low-config vertex count is bounded by maxConnections × segments × 2

tests.name: line positions exist in 3D space (Z values vary, not coplanar)
tests.description: The Bezier arcs push control points perpendicular to the pair axis, so positions must span all three dimensions.
assertions: At least some Z-coordinate values in the position attribute are non-zero
Position values are all finite (no NaN/Infinity)

tests.name: draw() does not throw with valid FrameState
tests.description: Basic smoke test that draw executes without error after init.

tests.name: draw() does not throw with edge-case params (zero bass, zero treble, no pointer)
tests.description: Edge-case frame parameters should not cause division by zero or other errors.
assertions: No exception thrown with bassEnergy=0, trebleEnergy=0, pointerDisturbance=0

tests.name: same positions produce same connection topology (deterministic)
tests.description: Two instances initialized with identical positions should produce the same activeVertexCount.
assertions: activeVertexCount from first init equals activeVertexCount from second init

tests.name: no localStorage or cookie access during init/draw operations
tests.description: Privacy requirement: the overlay must not use forbidden storage APIs.
assertions: localStorage.getItem spy not called during init/draw
document.cookie getter spy not called during init/draw

tests.name: BezierCurveWeb implements the Overlay interface (init, draw, cleanup, setOpacity, activeVertexCount)
tests.description: The system must satisfy the generic Overlay contract to be composable with any point-based geometry system.

tests.name: fog uniforms are present for depth fog integration
tests.description: The shader material should include fog-related uniforms for consistent scene compositing.
assertions: uFogNear or uFogFar uniform exists on the material

tests.name: boundary values (proximityThreshold=0, maxConnections=0) do not throw
tests.description: Zero-config edge cases should be handled gracefully without crashing.
assertions: init with proximityThreshold=0 does not throw
init with maxConnections=0 does not throw
